methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Thunder Tiger operates in Defense & Security at the public stage and has raised $22M+ as of 2025. The sector contains 19 same-stage peers, supplemented by a broader comp universe of 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Evolv Technology, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

Peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable here. The Defense & Security sector, while populated, lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum density thresholds—only 1 same-stage peer was identified against a requirement of 3 or more to support reliable median triangulation. Applying all-sector median multiples would introduce unacceptable selection bias given the heterogeneity of business models, technology maturity, and go-to-market stages within the defense technology landscape.

The analysis therefore cannot produce a defensible valuation range using standard comparable-company methods. Valuation work on Thunder Tiger would require alternative approaches: asset-based or revenue multiple analysis if financial statements are available, venture-backed cost-of-capital modeling given the $22M+ capital stack, or custom peer benchmarking against the identified private cohort (Anduril, Helsing, Shield AI) with detailed stage and revenue alignment. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and regulatory environment exposure—all material to defense-tech risk adjustment.
